ddls:
CREATE TABLE "transactions" (
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transaction record */,
  "transaction_type" TEXT /* Type of transaction (e.g., NEW_ORDER, DELIVERY) */,
  "duration_seconds" REAL /* Duration of the transaction processing in seconds */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Number of transactions processed */,
  "tpm" REAL /* Transactions per minute (TPM) rate */,
  "sum_ms" REAL /* Total processing time in milliseconds */,
  "avg_ms" REAL /* Average processing time in milliseconds */,
  "p50_ms" REAL /* 50th percentile latency in milliseconds */,
  "p90_ms" REAL /* 90th percentile latency in milliseconds */,
  "p95_ms" REAL /* 95th percentile latency in milliseconds */,
  "p99_ms" REAL /* 99th percentile latency in milliseconds */,
  "p999_ms" REAL /* 99.9th percentile latency in milliseconds */,
  "max_ms" REAL /* Maximum latency in milliseconds */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the transaction metrics were recorded */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the system table */,
  "error_count" INTEGER /* Number of transactions that resulted in errors */,
  "success_rate" REAL /* Percentage of successful transactions */,
  "retry_count" INTEGER /* Number of retries for the transaction */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the client or system initiating the transaction */,
  "destination_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the server or system processing the transaction */,
  "transaction_status" TEXT /* Status of the transaction (e.g., successful, failed, in progress) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES system ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_transaction_type FOREIGN KEY ("transaction_type") REFERENCES transaction_types ("type_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "transaction_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transaction type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the transaction type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the transaction type */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the system table */,
  "sla_thresholds" TEXT /* JSON column to store Service Level Agreement (SLA) thresholds for the transaction type (e.g., latency, throughput) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transaction_types_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES system ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the transaction record */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "metric_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the performance metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the metric was recorded */,
  "source_system" TEXT /* Name of the system where the metric was collected */,
  "destination_system" TEXT /* Name of the system where the transaction was processed */,
  "metric_source" TEXT /* Source of the metric (e.g., internal, external) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_transaction_id FOREIGN KEY ("transaction_id") REFERENCES transactions ("transaction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the transaction record */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., latency, throughput) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_source" TEXT /* Source of the alert (e.g., automated, manual) */,
  "alert_duration" REAL /* Duration for which the alert condition was active */,
  "alert_resolved" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_transaction_id FOREIGN KEY ("transaction_id") REFERENCES transactions ("transaction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, viewer) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., IT, Operations, Sales) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the transaction record being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the browser or tool used to access the data */,
  "access_duration" REAL /* Duration of the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_transaction_id FOREIGN KEY ("transaction_id") REFERENCES transactions ("transaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system event */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of event (e.g., maintenance, update) */,
  "event_description" TEXT /* Description of the event */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the event occurred */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the event */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the system table */,
  "event_duration" REAL /* Duration of the event */,
  "event_outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the event (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_events_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_events_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES system ("system_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system" (
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system */,
  "system_name" TEXT /* Name of the system */,
  "system_description" TEXT /* Description of the system */,
  "system_version" TEXT /* Version of the system */,
  "system_status" TEXT /* Status of the system (e.g., active, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_id")
)
